title: Bachelor In Medical Radiology and Imaging Technology
content: This program aims to provide the highest accredited educational and clinical experiences that will render qualified, patient-focused, compassionate, critical thinkers Medical Radiology and imaging technologists for the community. After completion of this course , one should be able to do image acquisition , and image processing and handle all radiological and imaging equipment independently or as a healthcare team member. They should also be able to ensure radiation protection , and quality assurance, identify and manage emergency situations, provide empathetic professional patient care XII with minimum 50% marks in Physics, Chemistry and Biology (PCB). The minimum age should be 17 years as on December 31st of each Admission Year. (Medical fitness certificate is mandatory to submit and it is to be endorsed by Government Medical Officer) 01 Clinical Care : Apply core basic knowledge associated with the Medical Radiology and Imaging Technology profession which includes Ethics, Good Lab Practice, Quality Control and Patient Safety. 02 Communication in Radiology and Imaging Technology Practice : Develop an effective communication with patients, care-givers, other health professional’s members of community and procedures to perform to make an investigation reports. 03 Membership of a multidisciplinary health team : Distinguish and communicate within the team, including transparency about aims, decisions, uncertainty and mistakes to work collaboratively and professionally to accomplish goal and accurate result for patient. 04 Ethics and accountability in medical lab Practice Apply ethical principles and commit to core concepts of clinical ethics and law to practice as healthcare service providers in the field of Medical Lab Technology. 05 Commitment to professional excellence Develop and execute professionalism to reflect in thought and action including technical competence, appearance, image, confidence level, empathy, compassion, understanding, patience, manners, verbal and non-verbal communication, an anti- discriminatory and non-judgmental attitude, and appropriate physical contact to ensure safe, effective and expected delivery of healthcare. 06 Leadership and mentorship Adapt leadership role where needed in order to ensure clinical significance and patient satisfaction and respond in an autonomous and confident manner to planned and uncertain errors or any other condition. 07 Social Accountability and Responsibility Assess and recognize that allied and healthcare professionals need to be advocates within the healthcare system, to judiciously manage resources. 08 Scientific attitude and Scholarship Adapt scientific and scholarly principles during interactions with patients and peers, educational endeavors, research activities and in all other aspects of professional lives. 09 Lifelong learning: Adapt the ability to engage in independent and lifelong learning committed to the patient’s care and patient’s health. 01 Provide the profession and community with trained qualified technologist. 02 Provide education a comprehensive program that promotes problem solving, critical thinking and communication skills in the clinical environment. 03 Students will demonstrate quality patient care skills including professionalism and ethical behaviors as specified in the code of ethics. 04 Graduate students with specific skills necessary to be competent entry level. The minimum age should be 17 years as on December 31st of each Admission Year. (Medical fitness certificate is mandatory to submit and it is to be endorsed by Government Medical Officer)
